violations.claim: VIOLATION: result.report.proven is typed 'any' — a negative proven count can propagate
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 498
; VIOLATION: result.report.proven is typed 'any' — a negative proven count can propagate
; to display. Semantically, a proven count must be >= 0 (Z3 "unsat" results counted).
; printSummary reads and displays the value with no validation.
(declare-const pipeline_result_proven Int)  ; value actually stored in result.report.proven
(declare-const displayed_proven Int)        ; value template-interpolated at line 498
; Code transition: printSummary binds displayed_proven = result.report.proven directly
(assert (= displayed_proven pipeline_result_proven))
; Code adds no constraint: no (>= 0) guard, no typeof check, no isNaN check
; Violation: a pipeline bug returns a negative count; printSummary propagates it verbatim
(assert (< pipeline_result_proven 0))
(assert (< displayed_proven 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Phase 3 proven (from reduce, line 497) and Phase 5 proven (result.report,
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 498
; VIOLATION: Phase 3 proven (from reduce, line 497) and Phase 5 proven (result.report,
; line 498) are independently sourced. The domain invariant is Phase5 <= Phase3
; (Z3 can only verify formulas that the LLM derived). printSummary never checks this.
(declare-const phase3_proven Int)  ; sum of c.proven.length from reduce at line 497
(declare-const phase5_proven Int)  ; result.report.proven read at line 498
; Code transition A: phase3_proven = contracts.reduce(sum proven.length, 0)
; Since each c.proven.length >= 0, the sum is non-negative
(assert (>= phase3_proven 0))
; Code transition B: phase5_proven is read from result.report (any-typed)
; No code in printSummary links phase5_proven to phase3_proven
; Violation: the summary can display Phase5 > Phase3 — internally contradictory
; (Z3 "verified" more properties than the LLM ever derived)
(assert (> phase5_proven phase3_proven))
(check-sat)
